title: malus princeps
intro: Malus princeps, also known as the wild apple or crabapple, is a type of shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). The medicinal properties of Malus princeps lie in its bark, leaves, and fruit, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. The health benefits of Malus princeps include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against oxidative stress. Malus princeps can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
